item.description:
ChainLink 0.3.539

Log rides by hand, import from Zwift & more, elegant PDF reports, and a smarter bike fit.

Log a ride — and import beyond FIT

  • Log a Ride: a new "＋ Add Ride" button on the Rides page lets you type a ride in by hand — duration, distance, a "Feel" scale (Easy → Max) that estimates your training load when there's no power or heart rate, plus an optional details drawer for power/HR/cadence/etc. It also edits an existing ride, so you can fix a bad import.
  • Import TCX, GPX and CSV, not just FIT: a Zwift or TrainerRoad TCX comes in complete (graphs, map and accurate TSS, like a FIT); a phone app's GPX and summary CSVs work too. Each lands in a review sheet, pre-filled, so you confirm or fill any gaps before it saves — and re-importing the same file won't duplicate it.
  • The Import window got a full visual refresh to match the rest of the app.

Elegant PDF reports

  • The build report and bike-fit sheet share one polished look — proper margins, page numbering, a date, and per-section subtotals. Choose A4 or US Letter in Settings. The fit sheet is now downloadable, not just shareable.
  • The build report shows both an actual cost (every part) and your cost to complete (minus parts already in hand), in a more compact layout.

A sharper bike fit

  • Measure saddle fore/aft from the 80 mm biomechanical point (where your sit bones load the saddle) instead of the fiddly nose — it's repeatable and survives a saddle swap. A guide helps you find the point, and the fit sheet prints an actual-size gauge.
  • No mental arithmetic: measurements that used to want a difference (setback, bar drop) now take two plain wall/floor readings and the app does the sum, showing its working.
  • Your height and inseam show on the fit page, and every fit warning was audited so it no longer cries wolf on a correct measurement.

AI coach

  • Fixed the coach failing to answer on newer Google Gemini models (a "thought signature" error) — the free Gemini option works again.

Also

  • The bike builder counts parts you need two of (rotors, tyres, valves…) — automatically, or set any quantity by hand.
  • The Rides toolbar is tidier on iPad, and cross-training activities in "Log a Ride" now follow the sports you enable in Settings.

ChainLink 0.3.511

A quieter guest on your Garmin account, and a full-history Rides graph.

Your Garmin account comes first

  • The ride-history import is now deliberately polite: left alone it waits while your latest ride uploads and settles, spreads big histories over a few days, and paces every request — with honest status lines in Settings so it never looks stuck.
  • In a hurry? Pressing Sync yourself imports without the daily limit — user-initiated is your call.
  • Settings > Garmin Connect gains a "Pause history import" switch; new rides keep syncing while it's paused.

Rides graph: the whole story

  • The Rides page graph now covers your entire history — switch to Years for the full picture, tap any bar to jump there. The list keeps its fast paged loading.

Fixed

  • The Fitness page's three sub-page cards (Power Profile, Courses, Performance Management) work again.
  • Training plans created mid-week count the whole week honestly and the plan page opens on the correct week.

ChainLink 0.3.507

Half the disk, clearer form numbers, and a stack of fixes.

Ride file archive: half the size

  • Original ride files are now stored compressed — on first launch this build quietly converts your existing archive, roughly halving the space it takes. Nothing changes in how the app behaves or performs.
  • Settings > Data now shows the archive's size and file count, so you can see what's on this device.

Form (TSB) finally speaks plainly

  • The Training and Fitness pages' metric pills carry a one-word verdict — Fresh / Neutral / Building / Overreached / Too Fresh — with honest colours: green only for genuine race freshness, amber for the productive fatigue of a training block, red only for real overreach. No more "red = you trained properly".
  • The charts' form shading follows the same bands, day by day — a hard block reads amber, and only the depth below −30 turns red.
  • The "What this means" readout now uses the same rounded numbers as the pills and names both terms ("Fitness (CTL) 28…").

Training plans: honest week accounting

  • Plans created mid-week no longer mis-count: the current week, the plan page's week picker and the compliance verdict all follow the plan's own Monday-anchored weeks. A big week no longer reads "behind plan" because most of it happened before you pressed Generate.

Fixed

  • Coach Insights cards expand on tap to show their full text (they were clamped to two lines).
  • Clicking an upcoming event on the Overview goes to the Events page — the editor only opens from the right-click "Edit in Events" action.
  • The Fitness page's three sub-page cards (Power Profile, Courses, Performance Management) work again on the Mac.
